explicit_text: My husband originally worked in Mississippi, earning just 30 cents an hour before he took a better-paying job at the Arsenal. Mississippi was where our family started before relocating to Ohio.

explicit_text: Windham, Ohio, was a small village that grew rapidly because of the Arsenal. When we moved there, the population had jumped from just over 300 to about 3000, with many new homes and a new elementary school. We lived in the Maple Grove Housing Project and later bought land outside the village to build our own home. Life in Windham was busy during the war years but slowed down afterward.

explicit_text: The Ravenna Arsenal was a massive government project started in 1940, covering 22,000 acres and employing over 18,000 workers in just ten months. It transformed Windham from a tiny village with no utilities into a booming town with water, sewage, and housing. My husband worked there after leaving Mississippi, and we lived in the housing projects built for Arsenal workers. Although the Arsenal is now obsolete, it played a huge role in the community and national defense.

explicit_text: The Maple Grove Housing Project was where we lived when my husband started working at the Arsenal. Built between 1942 and 1943, it had six types of apartment buildings with coal furnace heating and basic plumbing. Rent was affordable back then, and the project included a community building, a theatre, a library, and a school. Some of those buildings still stand today as low-cost housing.
